title: सूर्य स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सूर्य स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
जपाकुसुमसंकाशं काश्यपेयं महाद्युतिम्।
तमोऽरिं सर्वपापघ्नं प्रणतोऽस्मि दिवाकरम्॥

॥ स्तोत्रम् ॥

नमः सवित्रे जगदेकचक्षुषे
जगत्प्रसूतिस्थितिनाशहेतवे।
त्रयीमयाय त्रिगुणात्मधारिणे
विरञ्चिनारायणशंकरात्मने॥१॥

सूर्यो लोकस्य चक्षुर्महति तपति दिव्यमम्बरे तेजसा।
भूतानामात्मभूतो भुवनभवनकृत्सर्वलोकप्रदीपः।
कालो भूतानि कालः सकलजनयिता सर्वदेवात्मदेवो
भानुर्भानुप्रतापो भवतु मम सदा मङ्गलं भास्करो मे॥२॥

भानो भास्कर मार्तण्ड छन्दांशो दिवाकर।
अरुणे पद्मबन्धो सूर्य विवस्वन् प्रभाकर॥३॥

द्वादशात्मन् नमस्तुभ्यं प्राच्यां प्रत्यक् दिशां पते।
ज्योतिषां पतये तुभ्यं नमस्ते ब्रह्मवर्चसे॥४॥

सप्ताश्ववाहनं देवं कुण्डलद्वयभूषितम्।
रक्तपद्मासनासीनं पद्महस्तं दिवाकरम्॥५॥

सहस्रकिरणं देवं कमलासनमाश्रितम्।
ग्रहराजं दिवानाथं नमामि सविदं पतिम्॥६॥

आदिदेवो महादेवो दिव्यतेजसमन्वितः।
सर्वव्याधिहरो भानुः सर्वपापप्रणाशनः॥७॥

हृद्रोगं मम सूर्यश्च हरिमाणं च नाशयेत्।
शुक्लं च हरिद्राभं मत्तेजः सूर्य मे हर॥८॥

आरोग्यं भास्करादिच्छेद्बलमिच्छेच्च वासवात्।
वीर्यमिन्दोरुमेच्छेत्तु ज्ञानं भर्गात्सदोदयम्॥९॥

नमः सूर्याय शान्ताय सर्वरोगनिवारिणे।
आयुर्वेदमयस्वामिन् सर्वज्वरविनाशन॥१०॥

उदयत्वं सदा भानो सर्वसौभाग्यदायक।
सर्वदुःखप्रशमन सर्वपापप्रणाशन॥११॥

रश्मिभिस्तव सर्वेषां प्राणिनां प्राणरक्षणम्।
तमसो मोचनं दिव्यं प्रकाशं जगतां हित॥१२॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
सूर्यस्तोत्रमिदं दिव्यं सर्वपापप्रणाशनम्।
सूर्योदयसमये पाठात्सर्वव्याधिविनाशनम्॥

त्रिकालं यः पठेन्नित्यं दीर्घायुष्मान् भवेन्नरः।
सर्वारिष्टप्रशमनं सर्वसम्पत्प्रदायकम्॥

॥ इति श्री भविष्यपुराणोक्तं श्री सूर्य स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Surya Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Japa-kusuma-sankasham Kashyapeyam Mahadyutim.
Tamo'rim Sarva-papa-ghnam Pranato'smi Divakaram.

|| Stotram ||

Namah Savitre Jagad-eka-chakshushe
Jagat-prasuti-sthiti-nasha-hetave.
Trayi-mayaya Tri-guna-atma-dharine
Viranchi-Narayana-Shankara-atmane.||1||

Suryo Lokasya Chakshur-mahati Tapati Divyam-ambare Tejasa.
Bhutanam-atma-bhuto Bhuvana-bhavana-krit Sarva-loka-pradipah.
Kalo Bhutani Kalah Sakala-janayita Sarva-deva-atma-devo
Bhanur-bhanu-pratapo Bhavatu Mama Sada Mangalam Bhaskaro Me.||2||

Bhano Bhaskara Martanda Chhandamsho Divakara.
Arune Padma-bandho Surya Vivasvan Prabhakara.||3||

Dvadash-atman Namas-tubhyam Prachyam Pratyak Disham Pate.
Jyotisham Pataye Tubhyam Namaste Brahma-varchase.||4||

Sapta-ashva-vahanam Devam Kundala-dvaya-bhushitam.
Rakta-padma-asana-asinam Padma-hastam Divakaram.||5||

Sahasra-kiranam Devam Kamala-asanam-ashritam.
Graha-rajam Diva-natham Namami Savidam Patim.||6||

Adi-devo Mahadevo Divya-tejasa-manvitah.
Sarva-vyadhi-haro Bhanuh Sarva-papa-pranashanah.||7||

Hrid-rogam Mama Suryash-cha Harimanam cha Nashyet.
Shuklam cha Haridrrabham Mat-tejah Surya Me Hara.||8||

Arogyam Bhaskara-ad-ichchhed-balam-ichchhech-cha Vasavat.
Viryam-Indor-umech-chhettu Jnanam Bhargat-sadodayam.||9||

Namah Suryaya Shantaya Sarva-roga-nivarine.
Ayurveda-maya-svamin Sarva-jvara-vinashana.||10||

Udayat-tvam Sada Bhano Sarva-saubhagya-dayaka.
Sarva-duhkha-prashamana Sarva-papa-pranashana.||11||

Rashmibhis-tava Sarvesham Praninam Prana-rakshanam.
Tamaso Mochanam Divyam Prakasham Jagatam Hita.||12||

|| Phalashrutih ||
Surya-stotram-idam Divyam Sarva-papa-pranashanam.
Surya-udaya-samaye Pathat-sarva-vyadhi-vinashanam.

Tri-kalam Yah Pathen-nityam Dirgha-ayushman Bhaven-narah.
Sarva-arishta-prashamanam Sarva-sampat-pradayakam.

|| Iti Shri Bhavishya-puranoktam Shri Surya Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
ध्यान — जपाकुसुम (गुलाब/जवा) के समान कान्ति वाले, कश्यप के पुत्र, महाद्युतिमान, अन्धकार के शत्रु, सर्वपापनाशक दिवाकर सूर्य को मैं प्रणाम करता हूँ।

जगत के एकमात्र नेत्र, जगत की उत्पत्ति-स्थिति-नाश के कारण, त्रयी (तीन वेद) मय, त्रिगुणात्मक, ब्रह्मा-विष्णु-शंकर स्वरूप सवित्र को नमस्कार।

सूर्य लोक के नेत्र हैं, दिव्य आकाश में महान तेज से तपते हैं। प्राणियों के आत्मभूत, भुवन-भवन के रचयिता, सर्वलोकों के प्रदीप, काल, जनयिता, सर्वदेवात्मदेव — भानु मेरा सदा मंगल करें।

सात अश्वों के वाहन वाले, दो कुण्डलों से भूषित, रक्तपद्म पर आसीन, पद्महस्त दिवाकर, सहस्रकिरणी, ग्रहराज, दिवानाथ को मैं नमन करता हूँ।

आदिदेव, महादेव, दिव्यतेजस्वी, सर्वव्याधिहर भानु, सर्वपापनाशक। हृदय रोग और हरिमा (पीलापन/पाण्डुरोग) का नाश करें सूर्य।

सूर्योदय के समय इस स्तोत्र के पाठ से सभी व्याधियाँ नष्ट होती हैं। त्रिकाल पाठ करने वाला दीर्घायुष्मान होता है।
benefits: Comprehensive hymn to the Sun God for health, vitality, and longevity
Associated in tradition with heart diseases (hrid-roga), jaundice (harimana), and all fevers
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and removes darkness (ignorance) from life
Invokes health from surya, strength from indra, virility from chandra, knowledge from agni
Recitation at sunrise removes all diseases and afflictions
Three-times daily recitation grants long life and removes all calamities
description: Surya Stotram (सूर्य स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Sun God Surya. This sacred composition is sourced from Bhavishya Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to comprehensive hymn to the Sun God for health, vitality, and longevity and three-time daily recitation grants long life and remove all calamities. It is also said to grant health from Surya, strength from Indra, virility from Chandra, knowledge from Agni. For best results, devotees chant this on Sunday (Ravivar) and during Makar Sankranti, Ratha Saptami, and Chhath Puja. At 12 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 8 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Surya Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Surya Stotram from Bhavishya Purana?
seoFaqs.answer: The Surya Stotram (सूर्य स्तोत्रम्) is a 12-verse Sanskrit devotional hymn to Bhagavan Surya, the Sun God, preserved in the Bhavishya Purana — one of the eighteen Mahapuranas which devotes substantial sections to Surya upasana, Saura sampradaya and Magh-mahatmya. The stotra opens with the famous dhyana verse Japa-kusuma-sankasham Kashyapeyam Mahadyutim, Tamo-ari Sarva-papa-ghnam pranato-asmi Divakaram — I bow to Divakara, of red-japa-flower complexion, son of Kashyapa, of great splendour, enemy of darkness, destroyer of all sin. The hymn praises Surya as Jagad-eka-chakshu (the one eye of the universe), Trayi-maya (embodiment of the three Vedas), Viranchi-Narayana-Shankara-atmane (one with the trimurti), Sapta-ashva-vahana (seven-horse-charioted), Sahasra-kirana (thousand-rayed) and Graha-raja (king of planets). The text contains 12 verses preserved in classical Vedic tradition and is most associated with healing prayers — heart disease (hrid-roga), jaundice (harimana) and all fevers. (Source: Bhavishya Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Arogyam Bhaskarad ichchhet — the famous prescription verse?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 9 of the Surya Stotram is one of the most quoted shlokas in Hindu remedial astrology and Ayurvedic devotion: Arogyam Bhaskarad-ichchhed-balam-ichchhech-cha Vasavat, Viryam-Indor-um-ichchhettu jnanam Bhargat-sadodayam — One should pray for arogyam (perfect health) from Bhaskara (the Sun), for balam (strength) from Vasava (Indra), for viryam (vitality and virility) from Indu (the Moon) and for jnanam (knowledge) from Bharga (Agni / the wisdom-fire). This verse establishes Surya as the supreme presiding deity of arogya — physical and mental health — and is the scriptural basis for the daily Surya Arghya, Surya Namaskar and Aditya upasana practised across India. The verse is recited at the start of Ayurveda treatments, before surgery, and as part of grah-shanti rituals when the natal Sun is afflicted in the horoscope. (Source: Surya Stotram, Bhavishya Purana, verse 9.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Surya Stotram be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Surya Stotram is most powerful on Sunday (Ravivar — the day of Surya), Ratha Saptami (Magha Shukla Saptami — the Surya Jayanti / official birthday of Surya as the seven-horse charioteer), Makar Sankranti (Surya's transit into Capricorn — the start of Uttarayana), Chhath Puja (the four-day Surya festival of Bihar, Jharkhand and eastern UP), Samba Dashami, all Sankranti days, and during a Sun (Surya) Mahadasha or Antardasha in vimshottari dasha. The phala-shruti specifies Surya-udaya-samaye — at sunrise — as the ideal recitation time, with the practitioner facing east, ideally during the Surya Arghya offering of water from a copper vessel containing red flowers, akshata and roli. Tri-kalam yah pathen-nityam dirgh-ayushman bhaven-narah — one who recites three times daily (morning, noon, evening) attains long life. (Sources: Bhavishya Purana phala-shruti; Drikpanchang Surya festivals.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Surya Stotram grant according to the phalashruti?
seoFaqs.answer: The phala-shruti at the close of the Surya Stotram specifies the following blessings to sincere recitation: (1) Sarva-papa-pranashanam — destruction of all sins, (2) Surya-udaya-samaye pathat sarva-vyadhi-vinashanam — recitation at sunrise destroys all diseases, (3) Tri-kalam yah pathen-nityam dirgh-ayushman bhaven-narah — three-times daily recitation grants long life, (4) Sarva-arishta-prashamanam — pacification of all calamities and astrological afflictions, (5) Sarva-sampat-pradayakam — bestowal of every kind of wealth and prosperity, (6) cure for heart disease (hrid-roga), jaundice and pallor (harimana), all fevers (sarva-jvara) and skin disorders — the body parts ruled by the Sun, (7) Brahma-varchasa — Vedic spiritual lustre, (8) blessings for father, governmental authority, leadership and royal favour, and (9) tejasa — inner radiance and confidence. (Source: Surya Stotram phala-shruti, Bhavishya Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of the seven horses, twelve forms and thousand rays of Surya in this stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Surya Stotram packs the entire iconography of Surya into its verses. (1) Sapta-ashva-vahana — the seven horses pulling Surya's chariot represent the seven colours of sunlight (Newton's spectrum, known to Vedic seers as the seven rashmis), the seven days of the week, the seven svaras of music, the seven dhatus of the body, the seven lokas and the seven Vedic metres. (2) Dvadash-atman — the twelve aditya forms (Mitra, Aryaman, Bhaga, Varuna, Daksha, Amsha, Tvashta, Indra, Vivasvan, Pushan, Parjanya, Vishnu) presiding one over each solar month. (3) Sahasra-kirana — the thousand rays which are the Sun's infinite cosmic functions: nourishing crops, evaporating water, sustaining prana, illumining knowledge. (4) Kundala-dvaya — the two ear-rings made by Vishvakarma from the lopped-off Sun-disc which became Vishnu's chakra. (5) Rakta-padma-asana — the red-lotus seat symbolising the rising-sun aurora. (Sources: Bhavishya Purana; Surya Upanishad iconography.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Surya Stotram differ from Aditya Hridayam and Surya Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: All three are major Surya stotras but differ in source, length and emphasis. (1) The Aditya Hridayam is a 31-verse hymn from the Yuddha Kanda of the Valmiki Ramayana (sarga 105), taught to Sri Rama by sage Agastya on the battlefield before the slaying of Ravana — emphasising Surya as the supreme cosmic deity (sarva-deva-svarupa) and victory-giver in war and conflict. (2) The Surya Stotram (Bhavishya Purana, 12 verses) is the standard daily Surya upasana hymn — emphasising health (arogya), longevity (dirghayu) and removal of disease, with the famous Japa-kusuma-sankasham dhyana and the Arogyam Bhaskarad-ichchhet prescription verse. (3) The Surya Ashtakam is an 8-verse short hymn for daily Surya namaskar at sunrise. Many devotees recite the Aditya Hridayam for victory over enemies and obstacles, the Surya Stotram for daily health and the Surya Ashtakam during the actual sun salutation practice. (Sources: Valmiki Ramayana 6.105; Bhavishya Purana; Surya upasana tradition.)
